libhankel
  • Python API
  • View page source

Python API

libhankel.hankel_transform()

Compute the Hankel transform.

Parameters:

  • nu (int) – The order of bessel function, must be 0 or 1.

  • f (callable or str) – Either a function to hankel-transform or a string naming a built-in function, i.e. either of ‘gdab’, ‘broad_peak’, ‘sphere’.

  • x_arr (numpy.ndarray of float64) – The points at which to evaluate the Hankel transform of the function f.

  • f_params (numpy.ndarray of float64) – Input parameters needed by the function f (ordered).

  • strategy_name (str) – The name of Hankel strategy to use. Refer to the table in Strategy Parameters for a list of possible strategies.

  • strategy_params (dict[str, float | int]) – The parameters needed by the chosen strategy. See Strategy Selection or C API (strategy_params) for details.

Returns:

The hankel transform.

Return type:

numpy.ndarray of float64

Please refer to Python examples for examples on how to use this function with either a builtin form factor or a custom input function.
